# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Jenkins Pipeline that downloads build artifacts from external URLs provided as parameters and deploys them to production servers. This allows us to deploy pre-built artifacts from different sources (S3, Artifactory, HTTP servers) without rebuilding.
# Category: cicd_security

import jenkins.model.*
 
// Fetch the instance of Jenkins
Jenkins job = Jenkins.instance

// Pipeline section for 'Deploy Artifacts' task on build step  
pipeline('Artifact Deployment') {    // pipeline block starts here    
 stage ('Download Build artifacts from external URLS provided as parameters and deploy to production servers:')  // declare a new Stage with name "Build"     
 def urlList = []                    // define an empty list of URls (change this line based on how you've structured your params)   
 urlList.add('http://external-url1/path');   // add URLs to the 'list', one per branch    
  urls={ "URLS": [:] + Shudson.Util.recollect(null, Jenkins.instance.items[it], { ["params"].eachWithIndex {{ it -> urlList << item }}})()["PARAMS"]   // re-add 'params' from the current context   
  println urls               /* you can uncomment this line if need be */     
 for (url in [urls])             // loop over each URL    
 {       printf("URL=%s\n", url)          // debug output        echo "Testing ${name}"           return name; }  yield(id: item.getID(), value:'http://external-server1/artifact')      [0] + (yield([buildArtifactId]: 'app', buildNumber :'latest'))}
   catch (Exception e){             // handle exceptions if any occur       println "There was a problem ${e}" }  yield(id: item.getID(), value:'http://external-server1/artifact')      [0] + Shudson.Utilities.recollect({}, Jenkins, { ["params"].eachWithIndex {{ it -> urlList << (item).buildArtifactId }} })()["PARAMS"] }
    println urls  // you can uncomment this line if need be        return name;       def servers=[]      /* define an empty list of server names */   Server.list().each { printf("%s\n", it) };//print current Jenkins instance configuration to console} else{printf('Jenkins not running or inaccessible...') }
    stage ('Docker Image Push: ' + (urlList[0])) // declare a new Stage with name "Push Docker image"  Here we are assuming that the artifacts should be available at specified URL and after successful deployment, it will push this docker images to AWS Elastic Container Registry
    try {            printf("URL=%ss", url)               /* you can uncomment this line if need be */           // debug output         println "Testing ${name}"          return name; }  yield(id: item.getID(), value:'http://external-server1/artifact')      [0] + (yield([buildArtifactId]: 'app', buildNumber :'latest'))}
    catch {              printf("There was a problem ${e}" // handle exceptions if any occur          println "There was a problem" }  yield(id: item.getID(), value:'http://external-server1/artifact')      [0] + Shudson.Utilities.recollect({}, Jenkins, { ["params"].eachWithIndex {{ it -> urlList << (item).buildArtifactId }} })()["PARAMS"] 
   } // end of for loop    println "Completed successfully" */)     run('Docker Image Push to AWS ECR: ' + serverName[0]) /* call this Jenkins command, execute the specified jenkin's commands and then return its result or name (if any). Here we are assuming that Docker images should be available at Amazon Elastic Container Registry  */
} // end of stage block for `Deploy Artifact` task on build step   run('Push Image to AWS ECR: ' + serverName[0]) /* call this Jenkins command, execute the specified jenkin's commands and then return its result or name (if any). Here we are assuming that Docker images should be available at Amazon Elastic Container Registry  */
} // end of pipeline block for `Deploy Artifact` task on build step   }// End Jenkinsfile code
